description_long: This object is a large, elongated airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a series of windows along its sides, suggesting a commercial passenger aircraft. The wings are mounted low on the fuselage and extend outward with a slight sweep, and there are visible engine nacelles under each wing. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a classic tail design. The landing gear appears to be a tricycle configuration, with main gear under the wings and a nose gear at the front. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with a distinct canopy and multiple windows. The exterior is primarily light gray with some darker and metallic accents, and the surface appears hard and smooth.
description: A large, streamlined commercial airplane with low-mounted swept wings, underwing engines, and a conventional tail. The fuselage is light gray with multiple windows and a forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, low-mounted swept wings, and the tail section.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the nose, cockpit windows, and the forward placement of the cockpit, with wings extending outward.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the full wingspan, engine nacelles, and the tail assembly.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail section, vertical and horizontal stabilizers, and the trailing edges of the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with moderately swept wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows
